fileName: MCLN EX103 Manage Zones UI Configuration Guide.docx

title: MCLN EX103 Manage Zones UI Configuration Guide
fullText:
MCLN EX 103 Manage Zones UI Configuration Guide Table of Contents TOC \o "1-7" \z \u \h 1 GLOBAL EXTENSION CONTROL CODE SETUP PAGEREF _Toc376855101 \h 3 1.1 Enable System Code C-000 PAGEREF _Toc987216043 \h 3 2 Add EX103 Custom Code PAGEREF _Toc183097817 \h 5 3 EX103 Filter Configuration PAGEREF _Toc1866666641 \h 7 4 WMAMZ Permission PAGEREF _Toc545571482 \h 11 GLOBAL EXTENSION CONTROL CODE SETUP Enable System Code C-000 Navigate to the System Codes (Configuration) UI. If system code C-000 (Global Extension Control) does not yet exist, click Add. Populate the following information: Note: The checkbox “System code parameters exist” will auto populate upon completion of subsequent steps for adding parameters, and it is un-editable to the user. Click Save. Next, click the Code ID’s Action Button The System Code ID’s screen is displayed. Click ADD. Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE to save the Code ID. Next, Click the Parameters Action button located on the bottom right of the screen to Navigate back to System Code Parameters screen. System Code parameters Screen is displayed. Click ADD button. Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE. Click on the Parameters tab. Check the box to enable all custom functionality. Click SAVE. Add EX103 Custom Code Open System Codes UI C lick Add. Populate the following information : NOTE: Code Description- * EX103 Container Type/Outbound Rules for Zone Picking * Click Save. Next, click the Code ID’s Action Button The System Code ID’s screen is displayed. Click ADD. NOTE: Code Description and Code ID description - * EX103 Container Type/Outbound Rules for Zone Picking * Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE to save the Code ID. Next, Click the Parameters Action button located on the bottom right of the screen to Navigate back to System Code Parameters screen System Code parameters Screen is displayed. Click ADD button. Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE. Click on the Parameters tab. Enter valid Rule Name found in Outbound Rules UI Click SAVE. EX103 Filter Configuration Go to Filter Layout UI in WM. Click on add. Add the details as shown below. 3.1 Wave Object Type: WM_CUST_EX103_FILTER Field Position: 1 Required: false Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.waveNbr Fi e ld Label: Wave Operator Type: COMP Field Type: STRING Hibernate Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.waveNbr Operator: ‘ =’ Quick/Save Filter: Both 3.2- Again, C lick on add next filter configuration. (FLOOR) Object Type: WM_CUST_EX103_FILTER Field Position: 2 Required: false F ield Name: MCLNManageZoneData.floor Field Label: Floor Operator Type: COMP Field Type: STRING Hibernate Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.floor Operator: ‘ =’ Quick/Save Filter: Both 3. 3 - Again, click on add next filter configuration. (CONTAINER TYPE) Object Type: WM_CUST_EX103_FILTER Field Position: 4 Required: false Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.containerType Field Label: Container Type Operator Type: COMP Field Type: STRING Hibernate Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.containerType Operator: ‘ =’ Quick/Save Filter: Both 3. 4 - A g ain, click on add next filter configuration. (ZONE NUMBER) Object Type: WM_CUST_EX103_FILTER Field Position: 5 Required: false Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.zoneNumber Field Label: Zone Number Operator Type: COMP Field Type: NUMBER Hibernate Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.zoneNumber Operator: ‘ =’ Quick/Save Filter: Both WMAMZ Permission Login into MDA and open Permissions UI Select Add Copy details below Select Save Navigate to Roles UI and filter for desired Role Select EDIT Filter for Administer Manage Zone S elect check box to enable custom permission Select Save

If system code C-000 (Global Extension Control) does not yet exist, click Add. Populate the following information: Note: The checkbox “System code parameters exist” will auto populate upon completion of subsequent steps for adding parameters, and it is un-editable to the user. Click Save. Next, click the Code ID’s Action Button The System Code ID’s screen is displayed. Click ADD. Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE to save the Code ID. Next, Click the Parameters Action button located on the bottom right of the screen to Navigate back to System Code Parameters screen. System Code parameters Screen is displayed. Click ADD button. Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE. Click on the Parameters tab. Check the box to enable all custom functionality. Click SAVE. Add EX103 Custom Code Open System Codes UI C lick Add. Populate the following information : NOTE: Code Description- * EX103 Container Type/Outbound Rules for Zone Picking * Click Save. Next, click the Code ID’s Action Button The System Code ID’s screen is displayed. Click ADD. NOTE: Code Description and Code ID description - * EX103 Container Type/Outbound Rules for Zone Picking * Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE to save the Code ID. Next, Click the Parameters Action button located on the bottom right of the screen to Navigate back to System Code Parameters screen System Code parameters Screen is displayed. Click ADD button. Populate the following information provided above. Click SAVE. Click on the Parameters tab. Enter valid Rule Name found in Outbound Rules UI Click SAVE. EX103 Filter Configuration Go to Filter Layout UI in WM. Click on add. Add the details as shown below. 3.1 Wave Object Type: WM_CUST_EX103_FILTER Field Position: 1 Required: false Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.waveNbr Fi e ld Label: Wave Operator Type: COMP Field Type: STRING Hibernate Field Name: MCLNManageZoneData.waveNbr Operator: ‘ =’ Quick/Save Filter: Both 3.2- Again, C lick on add next filter configuration.
